# Annotation Garden

> The Annotation Garden builds tools that turn natural-language and image descriptions of
> experimental events into valid, machine-readable annotations. HEDit is its Hierarchical Event
> Descriptor (HED) annotator: a command-line tool and Python package.

## Start here
- [HEDit](https://github.com/Annotation-Garden/HEDit): natural language and images to HED annotations
- [Install](https://pypi.org/project/hedit/): `pip install hedit`

## Use HEDit
- Annotate: `hedit annotate "participant pressed a button" -o json`
- Annotate an image: `hedit annotate-image file.png -o json`
- Validate: `hedit validate "Sensory-event, Visual-presentation"`

## Related
- [HED](https://www.hedtags.org): the standard HEDit targets, with validators to check its output
- [BIDS](https://bids.neuroimaging.io): where HED annotations are used

## Notes for agents
- HEDit is a command-line tool and Python package; use `-o json` for machine-readable output.
- It needs an OpenRouter API key (`hedit init`). Always validate the generated HED before use.
